steps.title: Set up the cosine ratio
steps.content: In a right triangle, $\cos\theta=\dfrac{\text{adjacent}}{\text{hypotenuse}}$. From the figure, the side adjacent to angle $x^{\circ}$ is $11$, and the hypotenuse is $28$.

steps.title: Compute the value
steps.content:
Therefore:
$$\cos x^{\circ}=\frac{11}{28}$$
As a decimal, $\frac{11}{28}\approx0.3929$, and SAT-style rounded or truncated entries such as $0.3928$, $0.3929$, $0.392$, and $0.393$ are accepted.
